SourceTracker Decontamination

Gabriela Puerres

2025-07-02

Contaminant Identification with SourceTracker

R package ‘SourceTracker’ version 1.0.

1. Setting

Load Required Libraries

# SourceTracker Libraries
library (dplyr)
library (phyloseq)
library (vegan)
library (ggplot2)
library (MicrobiotaProcess)
library (tidyr)
library (data.table)
library(devtools)
library (pairwiseAdonis)
library (microbiome)
library (patchwork)


# Paths
path.rds      <- "~/Decontamination/Analysis/RDS"
path.figures  <- "~/Decontamination/Analysis/Figures"
path.results  <- "~/Decontamination/Analysis/Results"

source("~/Decontamination/Scripts/SourceTracker.r") # Load SourceTracker package

Assign control as source, samples as sink:

SourceTracker with Biome Samples (“Feces”) as Sink Environments.

Sink:

  • “Feces”
  • “Mock_A”
  • “Mock_B”

Source:

  • “Reagent_Control”
  • “Extraction_Control”
  • “Environmental_Control”

Load Phyloseq object and Metadata

# Load data
#Phyloseq Object after Decontam
ps.decontam <- readRDS(file.path(path.rds,
"ps.decontam.rds"))
ps.decontam
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21818 taxa and 162 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 162 samples by 98 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21818 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Label samples as source or sink
sample_data(ps.decontam)$SourceSink <- case_when(
  sample_data(ps.decontam)$Sample_Type %in% c("Reagent_Control", "Extraction_Control", "Environmental_Control") ~ "source",
  sample_data(ps.decontam)$Sample_Type %in% c("Feces", "Mock_A", "Mock_B") ~ "sink",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)


# Filter out NA (e.g. mock) from the ps object!
ps.decontam.st <- subset_samples(ps.decontam, !is.na(SourceSink))
ps.decontam.st # New phylosseq object withouth Mock Samples!
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21818 taxa and 162 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 162 samples by 99 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21818 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Prepare OTU matrix and metadata
otu_mat <- as.matrix(data.frame(otu_table(ps.decontam.st)))
meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps.decontam.st))
common_samples <- intersect(rownames(otu_mat), rownames(meta))

# Get table only for the common samples
otu_mat <- otu_mat[common_samples, , drop = FALSE]
meta <- meta[common_samples, , drop = FALSE]

# Check the otu table
dim(otu_mat) # 21818 (OTUs),   162 (Samples)
## [1]   162 21818
# Double-check that the mapping file and otu table had overlapping samples:
if(length(common_samples) <= 1) {
  message <- paste(sprintf('Error: there are %d sample ids in
 common '),'between the metadata file
 and data table')
  stop(message)
}

# Transpose if needed
if (any(rownames(otu_mat) %in% meta$Corrected_Sample_ID)) {
  otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)
}

2. Train the model

# Extract the source environments and source/sink indices
train.ix <- which(meta$SourceSink=='source')
test.ix <- which(meta$SourceSink=='sink')

# Check sink OTU table dimensions:
dim(otu_mat[test.ix, ]) # 142 157
## [1] 147 162
length(test.ix) # 142
## [1] 147
# Train the model
otu_train <- ceiling(otu_mat[train.ix, ])
otu_test  <- otu_mat[test.ix, , drop = FALSE]

# env
envs <- factor(meta$SourceSink[train.ix])
#Tune the alpha values using cross-validation (this is slow!)
alpha1 <- alpha2 <- 0.001
alpha1 # 0.001
## [1] 0.001
alpha2 # 0.001
## [1] 0.001
# Train SourceTracker object on training data
st <- sourcetracker(otu_train, envs, rarefaction_depth = NULL)  # Skip rarefaction
#Check for NA and convert:
otu_test <- otu_mat[test.ix, ]
otu_test[is.na(otu_test)] <- 0
otu_test <- ceiling(otu_test)
#Export Data
saveRDS(st, (file.path(path.rds, "st.rds")))
saveRDS(otu_mat, (file.path(path.rds, "otu_mat.rds")))
saveRDS(otu_test, (file.path(path.rds, "otu_test.rds")))
saveRDS(envs, (file.path(path.rds, "envs.rds")))
saveRDS(train.ix, (file.path(path.rds, "train.ix.rds")))
saveRDS(test.ix, (file.path(path.rds, "test.ix.rds")))
# Define auto rarefaction depth based on training sample read counts
rarefaction_auto <- min(rowSums(otu_mat[train.ix, ]))
rarefaction <- rarefaction_auto

# Filter test samples that have enough reads
row_sums <- rowSums(otu_mat[test.ix, ])
sufficient_reads <- row_sums >= rarefaction
filtered_test_ix <- test.ix[sufficient_reads]
otu_test_filtered <- otu_mat[filtered_test_ix, ]

# Detect number of cores
library(parallel)
n.cores <- detectCores() - 1  # Leave one core free

# Predict source proportions in parallel
#results <- mclapply(1:nrow(otu_test_filtered), function(i) {
#  sample_matrix <- otu_test_filtered[i, , drop = FALSE]
#  predict(st, sample_matrix,
#          alpha1 = alpha1,
#          alpha2 = alpha2,
#          full.results = TRUE,
#          rarefaction_depth = rarefaction)},
#mc.cores = n.cores)

# Save the results
#saveRDS(results, file.path(path.rds, "sourcetracker_results1.rds"))

results <- readRDS("~/Decontamination/Analysis/RDS/sourcetracker_results.rds")
class(results)
## [1] "list"

The RDS file generated after prediction is of type list, but for downstream analyses, the object must be of class sourcetracker.fit

# Combine the results that are a list of sourcetracker.fit objects
combine_sourcetracker_fits <- function(fit_list) {
  # assume every element has class "sourcetracker.fit" and same structure
  
  # 1) Extract one element to get dimension names
  template <- fit_list[[1]]
  
  # 2) Combine draws: an array [n_taxa x n_envs x n_samples]
  draws_list <- lapply(fit_list, `[[`, "draws")
  combined_draws <- abind::abind(draws_list, along = 3)
  
  # 3) Combine full.results: integer array [n_taxa x n_envs x n_features x n_samples]
  full_list <- lapply(fit_list, `[[`, "full.results")
  combined_full   <- abind::abind(full_list, along = 4)
  
  # 4) Combine proportions (1×n_envs per sample → bind by row)
  props_list <- lapply(fit_list, `[[`, "proportions")
  combined_props <- do.call(rbind, props_list)
  
  # 5) Combine proportions_sd the same way
  psd_list <- lapply(fit_list, `[[`, "proportions_sd")
  combined_psd  <- do.call(rbind, psd_list)
  
  # 6) train.envs and samplenames
  #   train.envs should be identical across fits
  train.envs    <- template$train.envs
  sample_names  <- unlist(lapply(fit_list, `[[`, "samplenames"))
  
  # 7) Re-assemble into one sourcetracker.fit
  results_st_out <- list(
    draws          = combined_draws,
    proportions    = combined_props,
    proportions_sd = combined_psd,
    train.envs     = train.envs,
    samplenames    = sample_names,
    full.results   = combined_full
  )
  class(results_st_out) <- "sourcetracker.fit"
  return(results_st_out)
}

library(abind)  
big_fit <- combine_sourcetracker_fits(results)

# Check
class(big_fit)        # should be "sourcetracker.fit"
## [1] "sourcetracker.fit"
dim(big_fit$draws)   # 10 x 4 x 162 = taxa × envs × samples
## [1]  10   4 142
nrow(big_fit$proportions)  # 162
## [1] 142
# Save the new RDS file
saveRDS(big_fit, file = file.path(path.rds, "combined_sourcetracker_fit.rds"))
# Update the variable with the merged data
results <- readRDS(big_fit, file = file.path(path.rds, "combined_sourcetracker_fit.rds"))
class(results) #sourcetracker.fit
## [1] "sourcetracker.fit"
#draws: [10 × 4 × 142] = shape (taxa × envs × samples=1)
#proportions:[142 × 4] = shape (samples × envs)
#proportions_sd:[142 × 4] = shape (samples × envs)
#train.envs: [4]
#samplenames: [142] 
#full.results: [10 × 4 × 21643 × 142] = shape (taxa × envs × features × samples)
# get average of full results across all runs of sourcetracker
res.mean <- apply(results$full.results,c(2,3,4),mean)

# Get depth of each sample for relative abundance calculation
sample.depths <- apply(results$full.results[1,,,,drop=F],4,sum)

# Create directory to store outcomes
base_dir <- "~Analysis/Results"
outdir <- "Results_sourcetracker_cow"
filebase <- "ST_Cow_samples"
subdir <- file.path(base_dir, outdir, "full_results")
dir.create(subdir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
# write each environment as a separate file
for(i in 1:length(results$train.envs)){
  env.name <- results$train.envs[i]
  filename.fractions <- sprintf('%s/%s_%s_contributions.txt', subdir, filebase, env.name)
  res.mean.i <- res.mean[i,,]
  # handle the case where there is only one sink sample
  if(is.null(dim(res.mean.i))) res.mean.i <- matrix(res.mean.i,ncol=1)
  
  # make rows be samples, columns be features
  res.mean.i <- t(res.mean.i)
  
  # ensure proper names are retained
  colnames(res.mean.i) <- colnames(otu_mat)[1:ncol(res.mean.i)]
  rownames(res.mean.i) <- results$samplenames
  
  # calculate and save relative abundance
  res.mean.i.ra <- sweep(res.mean.i,1,sample.depths,'/')
  sink(filename.fractions)
  cat('SampleID\t')
  write.table(res.mean.i.ra,quote=F,sep='\t')
  sink(NULL)
}
#generate summary plots
if(dim(results$draws)[2] > 1) {
  plot.types <- c('pie')
} else plot.types <- c('pie', 'bar')
envs<-meta[rownames(results$proportions),'Type']
envs<-unlist(envs)
envs <- as.factor(envs)
labels = sprintf('%s_%s',envs, rownames(results$proportions))
plotixs <- sort(as.numeric(envs),index=TRUE)$ix
for(plot.type in plot.types){
  # plot each environment separately
  for(env in unique(envs)){
    plotixs <- which(envs == env)
    png(sprintf('%s/%s_%s_%s.png',outdir,filebase,plot.type,env),width=500,height=500)
    plot(results, type=plot.type, labels=labels, include.legend=TRUE, indices=plotixs)
    dev.off()    
  }
}

saveRDS(results, file.path(path.rds, "sourcetracker_contaminants_results.rds"))

3. Check SourceTracker object

# read phyloseq object after decontam
ps.decontam <- readRDS(file.path(path.rds,
"ps.decontam.rds"))
ps.decontam
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21897 taxa and 162 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 162 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21897 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
target <- "Feces"
ps.subset.feces <- subset_samples(ps.decontam, Sample_Type %in% target)
ps.subset.feces
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21897 taxa and 142 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 142 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21897 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]

4. Remove contaminants from targeted animal samples (“Feces”) using negative controls as source

# Define the path to the contaminants folder
path.contaminants <- file.path(path.results, "Results_sourcetracker_cow/full_results")

# Assign environment using full path
Environmental_Control <- as.data.frame(
  fread(file.path(path.contaminants, "ST_Cow_samples_Environmental_Control_contributions.txt")))
rownames(Environmental_Control) <- Environmental_Control[, 1]
Environmental_Control[, 1] <- NULL


Extraction_Control <- as.data.frame(fread(file.path(path.contaminants,'ST_Cow_samples_Extraction_Control_contributions.txt')))
row.names(Extraction_Control) <- Extraction_Control[,1]
Extraction_Control[,1] <- NULL


Reagent_Control <- as.data.frame(fread(file.path(path.contaminants,'ST_Cow_samples_Reagent_Control_contributions.txt')))
row.names(Reagent_Control) <- Reagent_Control[,1]
Reagent_Control[,1] <- NULL


Unknown <- as.data.frame(fread(file.path(path.contaminants,'ST_Cow_samples_Unknown_contributions.txt')))
row.names(Unknown) <- Unknown[,1]
Unknown[,1] <- NULL

5. Create Phyloseq object

a. Create Phyloseq object for contaminants identified by SourceTracker

# Summing the contaminant fractions
contam_sum <- Environmental_Control + Extraction_Control +  Reagent_Control + Unknown
dim(contam_sum) #  142 obs. of 21643 variables
## [1]   142 21643
# Total reads per sample
sample.depths <- sample_sums(ps.subset.feces)

# Create the otu table for contaminants by multiplying sample depths. From contaminant fractions to contaminant counts
otu_contam <- sweep(contam_sum,1,sample.depths,'*') %>% round(digits=0) 
dim(otu_contam) # 142 obs. of 21643 variables
## [1]   142 21643
# Variables for the Ps object:
# 1. Meta Data
meta.contam.st <- sample_data(ps.subset.feces)


# Taxa/OTU names should not match.
# 2. Sequences Data
seq.contam.st <- otu_table(otu_contam, taxa_are_rows = FALSE)
asv_ids_seq <- paste0("ASV", seq_len(ncol(seq.contam.st)))
colnames(seq.contam.st) <- asv_ids_seq
dimnames(seq.contam.st)[[2]][1:5]
## [1] "ASV1" "ASV2" "ASV3" "ASV4" "ASV5"
# 3. Taxa Data:
tax.contam.st <- phyloseq::tax_table(ps.subset.feces)
asv_seqs <- taxa_names(ps.subset.feces)
asv_ids <- paste0("ASV", seq_along(asv_seqs)) # Rename taxa in OTU table
taxa_names(ps.subset.feces) <- asv_ids
tax.contam.st <- phyloseq::tax_table(ps.subset.feces)
dimnames(tax.contam.st)[[1]][1:5]
## [1] "ASV1" "ASV2" "ASV3" "ASV4" "ASV5"
# Phyloseq Object for contaminants
ps.contam.st.checked <- phyloseq(seq.contam.st, tax.contam.st, meta.contam.st)
ps.contam.st.checked
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21643 taxa and 142 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 142 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21643 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Save the rds file
saveRDS(ps.contam.st.checked, file.path(path.rds,"ps.contam.st.rds"))

b. Create Phyloseq object for non-contaminants after SourceTracker

otu_noncontam <- sweep(Unknown,1,sample.depths,'*') %>% round(digits=0)

seq.noncontam <- otu_table(otu_noncontam, taxa_are_rows = FALSE)
asv_ids_seq.noncontam <- paste0("ASV", seq_len(ncol(seq.noncontam)))
colnames(seq.noncontam) <- asv_ids_seq.noncontam
dimnames(seq.noncontam)[[2]][1:5]
## [1] "ASV1" "ASV2" "ASV3" "ASV4" "ASV5"
# Phyloseq Object for non contaminants
ps.decontam.st <- phyloseq(seq.noncontam, tax.contam.st, meta.contam.st)
ps.decontam.st
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21643 taxa and 142 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 142 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21643 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Save the rds file
saveRDS(ps.decontam.st, file.path(path.rds,"ps.decontam.st.rds"))

6. Plots

a. Plot proportion of contaminants

meta <- sample_data(ps.subset.feces) %>% data.frame()

# proportion of contaminants is stored at results$proportions

prop.type <- results$proportions %>% data.frame()
prop.type$Sample_Type <- meta$Sample_Type
prop.type.long <- prop.type %>% pivot_longer(-Sample_Type, names_to = "Source", values_to = "Proportion")

prop <- results$proportions %>% data.frame()
prop$Sample_ID <- meta$Corrected_Sample_ID 

prop.long <- prop %>% pivot_longer(-Sample_ID,names_to="Source",values_to="Proportion")
prop.long$Sample_Type <- prop.type.long$Sample_Type

type_colors <-
  c(
    "Extraction_Control" = "#1f77b4",
    "Environmental_Control" = "#ff7f0e",
    "Reagent_Control" = "#d62728",
    "Unknown" = "#7AA874"
  )

p.prop.contam <- ggplot(data=prop.long, mapping = aes(x = Sample_ID, y = Proportion, fill = Source))+
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
  facet_wrap(~Sample_Type, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
        axis.title.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())+
  scale_fill_manual(values = type_colors)

p.prop.contam

b. Plot proportion of contaminants 2: Expanding upper segment

# install.packages("ggbreak")  # if not already installed
library(ggbreak)
## ggbreak v0.1.5 Learn more at https://yulab-smu.top/
## If you use ggbreak in published research, please cite the following
## paper:
## 
## S Xu, M Chen, T Feng, L Zhan, L Zhou, G Yu. Use ggbreak to effectively
## utilize plotting space to deal with large datasets and outliers.
## Frontiers in Genetics. 2021, 12:774846. doi: 10.3389/fgene.2021.774846
p.prop.contam.2 <- ggplot(data=prop.long, aes(x = Sample_ID, y = Proportion, fill = Source)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
  facet_wrap(~Sample_Type, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = type_colors) +
  scale_y_break(c(0.90, 0.90), scales = 0.5)  # adjust the range and scale factor as needed
p.prop.contam.2

c. Plot abundance of contaminants

ps.contam.st.checked
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 21643 taxa and 142 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 142 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 21643 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
ps.contam.st.melt <- psmelt(ps.contam.st.checked)
detection <- sum(taxa_sums(ps.contam.st.checked))/55

# genera with <5% relative abundance and <25% prevalence were grouped to Others
pseq.contam <- aggregate_rare(ps.contam.st.checked, level="Genus", detection = detection*0.05, prevalence = 0.25)

melt.pseq.contam <- psmelt(pseq.contam)
p.contam <-  ggplot(melt.pseq.contam, aes(x = Corrected_Sample_ID, y = Abundance, fill = Genus)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
  facet_wrap(~Sample_Type, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_bw()+
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    legend.key.size = unit(10, "pt"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )+
  scale_fill_brewer("Genus", palette = "Paired")
p.contam

d. Merge plots

p.contam.st <- ((p.prop.contam.2) / (p.contam)) + plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
p.contam.st

ggsave(file.path(path.figures, "Figure 4. sourcetracker_contaminants_per_sample.png"), p.contam.st, width = 12, height = 10, dpi=600)
